results.title: The Sami cooperative herding group: siida system
results.judge_reason: Pure source-domain anthropology article about Sami siida; no LLM/inference content so it cannot match the cross-domain candidate analogy.

results.title: Sami Norway — ICR
results.judge_reason: Source-side Sami herding description with two source-side keyword overlaps but no LLM-application linkage.

results.title: Sami Reindeer Herding Norway
results.judge_reason: Source-side description of 400 km autumn migration; only one content-word overlap and no LLM/inference relevance.

results.title: SkyLB: Cross-Region Load Balancer for LLM Inference (2025/5)
results.judge_reason: Cross-region load balancing using diurnal patterns is related but does not implement explicit annual-cycle migration with leader-follower herd cohesion or cooperative siida controllers.

results.title: SageServe (2025/2)
results.judge_reason: ARIMA-driven diurnal forecasting + spot-instance switching is adjacent to compute-zone migration but lacks cyclical scheduled migration and leader-follower cohesion semantics.

results.title: Agent.xpu Scheduling Agentic LLM on Heterogeneous SoC (2025/6)
results.judge_reason: Proactive/reactive daemon scheduling on SoC differs from annual-cycle inter-zone migration; novelty claim already cites this paper as distinct.

results.title: Context-Folding Long-Horizon LLM Agent (2025/10)
results.judge_reason: Context-folding is orthogonal to compute-zone migration and seasonal scheduling.

results.title: Scheduler-Theoretic Framework for LLM Agent Execution (2026/4)
results.judge_reason: DAG sub-goal scheduling does not implement cyclical fleet-zone migration or herd-cohesion routing.

notes: Result 2 (reindeerherding.org/sami-norway) trips the keyword-overlap trigger with two source-side content words (summer pasture, siida cooperative). Although it is a pure source-domain page with no LLM linkage and functional/semantic scores are low, the strict OR rule treats kw>=2 as a hit. LLM-side results (4,5) are adjacent but stay below all three thresholds. Per pipeline rule total_hits>=1 -> FAIL.
